commit.message:
fix(servicebus): resolve TOCTOU race in AmqpSender batch size (#56301) (#57097)

Replace separate MaxMessageSize/MaxBatchSize auto-properties with an
immutable LinkLimits snapshot swapped via a single volatile reference.
This eliminates the race window where concurrent callers could observe
a stale MaxBatchSize (1 MiB constructor default) after MaxMessageSize
was already updated from link metadata (262 KB on Standard tier).

Changes:
- Add private sealed class LinkLimits bundling MaxMessageSize + MaxBatchSize
- Use volatile LinkLimits _linkLimits with atomic reference swap
- Update CreateMessageBatchInternalAsync to use snapshot with local capture
- Update SendBatchInternalAsync with explicit null-safe size check
- Add defensive null check after GetOrCreateAsync for link creation failure
- Add 5 new unit tests (concurrency snapshot, range assertion, send-path
  size-exceeded, defensive null check, DefaultMaxBatchSize fallback)

Fixes Azure/azure-sdk-for-net#56301

Co-authored-by: Eldert Grootenboer (from Dev Box) <egrootenboer@microsoft.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: Phase 1B — canonical streaming integration via InferenceService.executeStream()

- Add StreamInferenceRequest and StreamInferenceResult types to shared/inferenceProviderTypes.ts
- Add InferenceService.executeStream() as the authoritative streaming entry point:
  - Emits stream lifecycle telemetry (stream_opened, stream_completed, stream_aborted)
  - Emits inference lifecycle telemetry (inference_started, inference_completed, inference_failed/timeout)
  - Reports ReflectionEngine signals for failures, timeouts, and degraded fallbacks
  - Implements bounded fallback (only when stream never opened; no retry after partial output)
- Update AgentService.streamWithBrain() to route through InferenceService.executeStream()
  when a provider selection is available; preserves direct brain fallback otherwise
- Add CanonicalStreaming.test.ts with 8 tests covering all stream lifecycle scenarios

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Release v2.32.2

- harden native LXC auto-update with staged rollback and detached runner
- add GitHub Pages stats dashboard and richer traffic archival

commit.message:
Major gameplay overhaul: combat, economy, AI, UI, and documentation

Gameplay mechanics:
- Lanchester combat: overwhelming force gets √ advantage
- Concerted attack bonus: multi-wave arrivals within 800ms coordinate (+12%/wave)
- Solo attack penalty: single waves are 85% effective
- Planet capacity/food system: production stops at capacity, excess forces decay logarithmically
- Territory overstretch: owning >6 planets accelerates decay
- Desperation bonus: ≤2 planets = 2× production
- Production under siege: incoming threats suppress production proportionally
- Forces reinforce friendly planets (was silently destroying them)
- Routes cleared on planet capture

AI improvements:
- AI now uses visible supply routes (same system as human)
- Dynamic interval: panic when losing, never truly relaxes when winning
- AI count scales with difficulty: Easy=1, Medium=2, Hard=3
- Capacity pressure: AI forced to attack when near cap

Visual polish:
- 3-layer parallax background with rotation
- Distant Magellanic galaxy clouds
- Marching ants on supply routes
- Orbitron font for force counts, scaled to planet size
- Impact flash rings on hostile arrivals
- Force stream width scales logarithmically with amount
- Capacity arc dims when production siege-suppressed
- Drag arrow with snap targeting and red invalid indicator

UI features:
- Difficulty selection menu (1/2/3 keys or click)
- Box-select: drag empty space to select planets, click target
- Smart routing: BFS pathfinding for out-of-range targets
- Shift+drag box-select to cancel routes
- Touch: virtual shift button for cancel mode
- Pause (Space), tutorial hint auto-hides
- Planet size variance tightened (15-30, was 15-40)

Documentation:
- Full JSDoc on all functions and classes
- README.md with complete game logic flow
- All tuning constants documented in constructor

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Major gameplay overhaul: parallax background, combat mechanics, capacity system, AI improvements, level system planning

- 3-layer parallax starfield with rotation (deep stars, dust/nebula, close stars with twinkle)
- Distant galaxy clouds (Magellanic-type smudges)
- Lanchester-style combat: overwhelming force advantage via sqrt scaling
- Planet capacity system: production stops at food cap, forces decay above max
- Visual capacity arc indicator (green/yellow/red)
- AI late-game fix: reduced complacency, lower attack threshold, scaled active planets
- Forces move one-way only (no reinforcement on arrival)
- Pause with Space key
- Pace slowed ~15% (force speed, production, AI intervals)
- Level design document (LEVELS.md): phenomena, difficulty curve, force types
- LLM strategic advisor spec (instructions/): Ollama/qwen3:4b integration
- Copilot instructions (.github/copilot-instructions.md)
- Implementation plan (plan.md)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

perf: analyze and optimize utility functions in src/plugins/utils (#8081)

* Initial plan

* docs: add performance analysis table for src/plugins/utils functions

Co-authored-by: pubkey <8926560+pubkey@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: fix consistency in usage counts across performance table

Co-authored-by: pubkey <8926560+pubkey@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: address code review feedback on PERFORMANCE.md consistency

Co-authored-by: pubkey <8926560+pubkey@users.noreply.github.com>

* perf: optimize HIGH-classified utility functions with benchmarks

- overwriteGetterForCaching: value descriptor instead of getter (~37% faster reads)
- deepEqual: merge two loops into single pass (~17% faster for unequal objects)
- getFromMapOrThrow: === undefined instead of typeof (~6% faster)

Co-authored-by: pubkey <8926560+pubkey@users.noreply.github.com>

* perf: optimize CRITICAL-classified utility functions with benchmarks

- flatClone: spread { ...obj } instead of Object.assign (~319% faster)
- getProperty: fast path for dot-notation avoids getPathSegments (~281% faster)
- objectPathMonad: unrolled 2-segment fast path (~332% faster)
- getFromMapOrCreate: === undefined instead of typeof (~6% faster)
- deepClone: collapsed null check, moved Blob after Array check

Co-authored-by: pubkey <8926560+pubkey@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Default karma to Chrome-only locally, all browsers in CI (#8082)

* Initial plan

* feat: default to Chrome only in karma config, run all browsers in CI

In config/karma.conf.cjs, the postDetection callback now checks
process.env.CI to determine which browsers to use:
- Local development (no CI env): only Chrome for faster runs
- GitHub CI (CI=true): detect and run all available browsers
The --browsers CLI arg override is preserved in both cases.

commit.message:
feat: implement full enterprise SSO/Auth microservice

- Multi-tenant schema: tenants, organizations, branches, locations,
  departments, users (tenant-scoped), devices, revoked_tokens,
  audit_logs (immutable), outbox_events
- Published Spatie Permission and Passport migrations
- Domain models with UUIDs, soft deletes, immutable AuditLog
- Repository pattern: interfaces + Eloquent implementations
  bound via RepositoryServiceProvider
- Services: AuthService, TokenService, RevocationService (Cache-based),
  AuditService, UserService
- HTTP: LoginRequest, RegisterRequest, RefreshTokenRequest,
  UserResource, TokenResource, DeviceResource, SuspiciousActivityDetection
- Controllers: AuthController, DeviceController, UserController
- Routes: /api/v1/auth/* (login/register/logout/logout-all/refresh/me),
  /api/v1/user/profile, /api/v1/devices, /api/health
- api guard (Passport), rate limiters (auth: 10/min, api: 60/min)
- Dockerfile, docker-compose.yml (app/mysql/redis/horizon), phpstan.neon
- 16 feature tests passing: Login, Logout, Register, TenantIsolation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: KV_SSO
repository.description: Multi-tenant SaaS Inventory Management System with Laravel microservices and React frontend, featuring Laravel Passport SSO, RBAC/ABAC authorization, modular architecture, and event-driven communication. Supports CRUD, tenant-aware access, advanced APIs, webhooks, and cross-service transactional consistency.

commit.message:
feat: Phase 22 - Production security hardening

- Rate limiting: general API (100/15min), login (10/15min), register (5/hr), upload (20/15min)
- Zod validation on review and favorite controllers
- AppError class + improved global error handler (no stack in production)
- Structured logger (JSON in prod, readable in dev) replacing all console.error
- Request logging middleware (method, URL, status, duration, IP)
- Helmet configured (CSP in prod, crossOriginResourcePolicy)
- Strict CORS (explicit methods, headers, maxAge)
- Env validation at startup via Zod (fails fast on missing vars)
- Health route: DB check, memory usage, node version, 503 on DB failure
- DB connection retry (5 attempts, exponential backoff)
- Socket.io auth middleware (rejects unauthenticated connections)
- Socket event data validation
- Auth middleware blocks users with status=blocked
- Role guard rejects unapproved vendors
- Body parser limit reduced from 10MB to 2MB (uploads use multer)
- Validate middleware helper for reusable route-level validation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: Phase 21 - Reviews, favorites, pagination, stats, search

Backend:
- Add Review model (rating 1-5, comment, unique per user+service)
- Add Favorite model (unique per user+service)
- POST/GET/DELETE /reviews endpoints
- POST/GET/DELETE /favorites + GET /favorites/ids
- Pagination + search for admin users and vendors
- Enhanced admin stats (pending counts, recent activity, reviews)
- Dashboard stats endpoint (GET /auth/dashboard-stats)
- Services search now includes vendor name/company

Frontend:
- StarRating component (interactive + readonly)
- ReviewSection on service detail page
- FavoriteButton (heart toggle) on service detail page
- Favorites dashboard page with pagination
- Reusable Pagination and StatsCard components
- Services page: search input + proper pagination metadata
- Dashboard home: live stats for vendor/user roles
- Admin home: enhanced stats with pending/recent counts
- Admin users/vendors: search + pagination
- Dashboard requests: pagination
